ddls:
CREATE TABLE "elections" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each election record */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the election */,
  "election_type" TEXT /* Type of election (presidential, congressional, etc.) */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the election */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "candidates" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each candidate record */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the candidate */,
  "party" TEXT /* Party affiliation of the candidate */,
  "incumbent" INTEGER /* Whether the candidate is an incumbent */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "election_results" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each election result record */,
  "election_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the elections table */,
  "candidate_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the candidates table */,
  "vote_share" REAL /* Vote share of the candidate in the election (%) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "economic_indicators" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each economic indicator record */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the economic indicator */,
  "average_recent_growth_personal_incomes" REAL /* Average recent growth in personal incomes (%) */,
  "us_military_fatalities_per_million" REAL /* US military fatalities per million population */,
  "us_total_nonfarm_payrolls" INTEGER /* Total nonfarm payrolls in thousands */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "election_forecasts" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each election forecast record */,
  "election_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the elections table */,
  "mean_baseline" REAL /* Mean baseline for electoral forecasts */,
  "error" REAL /* Error in predictions */,
  "absolute_error" REAL /* Absolute error in predictions */,
  "linear_regression_two_features" REAL /* Result of a linear regression analysis considering two features */,
  "pred_mse" REAL /* Predicted mean squared error from the model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "researchers" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each researcher record */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the researcher */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the researcher */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the researcher (admin, analyst, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report record */,
  "researcher_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the researchers table */,
  "election_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the elections table */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "report_text" TEXT /* Text of the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)
